articles.title: Spain's Leader Says No to War as US President Threatens Trade
articles.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said no to war in a speech after US President Donald Trump threatened to stop all trade with Spain. Trump is angry because Spain won't let the US use military bases in Spain to attack Iran. Sánchez talked about wars in Ukraine, Gaza, and Iraq, saying wars hurt people and break international laws. He wants peace instead. Spain has been critical of Israel's actions in Gaza and supports Palestine. Many Spanish people agree with Sánchez because they remember the unpopular Iraq War. The Spanish government is planning ways to help people if the trade threat affects them.

articles.title: British People Fly Home from the Middle East
articles.summary: Some British people stuck in the Middle East are getting help to fly home. A special plane will leave Oman tonight to take them back to the UK. This is because there are attacks in the area that have stopped many flights. Over 130,000 British people want to leave since a war started on Saturday. The UK government is trying to help the most vulnerable people first, like families with children. But there are not enough seats on the plane for everyone. People in places like Dubai are told to go to Oman to catch the flight. The government says they will find more ways to bring people home safely. Some people are frustrated because they do not have enough information about how to get home.

articles.title: Saharan Dust Makes Colorful Sunsets in the UK
articles.summary: A big cloud of dust from the Sahara Desert in Africa is coming to the UK this week. This dust is made of tiny sand and mineral pieces that travel far in the air. When it reaches the UK, it will make sunrises and sunsets look very beautiful with colors like gold, orange, and amber. But there is a downside: if it rains, the dust can mix with water and make dirty spots on cars and windows, called 'blood rain.' The best time to see the bright colors is on Thursday and Friday, especially in England and Wales when the sky is clear. The dust floats high up and scatters sunlight to create these pretty colors.

articles.title: Harry Styles' New Music Adventure
articles.summary: Harry Styles is a famous singer who used to be in a band called One Direction. After touring a lot, he took a break to think about his life and turned 30. He went to Italy, started running marathons, and wanted to love music again by watching other bands like LCD Soundsystem. His new album is called Kiss All The Time, Disco Occasionally, but it's not really about kissing or disco. It has funky dance music with heavy bass and drums, inspired by bands he likes. The music sounds happy, but his singing and words show he feels unsure sometimes. This album is about finding joy in music while dealing with mixed feelings.

articles.title: Minnesota Investigates Federal Officers for Misconduct
articles.summary: In Minnesota, a prosecutor named Mary Moriarty is investigating federal immigration officers for bad behavior. These officers, including a leader named Greg Bovino, are accused of using smoke canisters and chemical irritants during protests and near a school. The prosecutor says this caused harm to the community. The federal government disagrees, saying states cannot charge federal officers because they are protected by law. Bovino is known for aggressive tactics in cities like Minneapolis and Chicago. He was removed from his role after two people were shot by federal officers, leading to protests. The investigation looks at 17 cases to see if charges should be filed.

articles.title: France Moves Its Big Ship to Help Friends
articles.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has ordered a special ship called the Charles de Gaulle to move from the Baltic Sea to the Mediterranean Sea. This ship is an aircraft carrier, which means it carries airplanes, and it uses nuclear power. Macron wants to help protect friends and allies during a war in the Middle East. He said France has already shot down some drones to defend its allies. The ship will be protected by other smaller ships called frigates. France is not part of the recent attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel, but it is ready to defend itself and others. Macron also talked about helping countries like Cyprus, which is in the European Union. He wants the fighting to stop and for everyone to talk peacefully to find a solution.
